entries.summary: US House Speaker Nancy Pelosi arrived in Taipei, defying Beijing's warnings against the visit.
China's military launched live-fire drills around Taiwan after Pelosi departed Taipei.
China extended military exercises around Taiwan beyond their announced end date.
Taiwan said it shot down an unidentified civilian drone that crossed near Kinmen.
Taiwan's President Tsai Ing-wen met Speaker Kevin McCarthy in California, drawing Beijing's protest.
China launched three-day Joint Sword exercises encircling Taiwan after the McCarthy meeting.
China sent dozens of warplanes near Taiwan after Vice President Lai's US transit.
Lai Ching-te won Taiwan's presidential election, his Democratic Progressive Party's third term.
Nauru cut ties with Taiwan and switched recognition to Beijing days after Lai's win.
Two Chinese fishermen drowned during a Taiwan coast guard chase off Kinmen island.
A 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Taiwan's east coast, killing at least nine people.
Lai Ching-te was inaugurated as Taiwan's president, calling for peace with Beijing.
China launched two-day Joint Sword 2024A drills in punishment for Lai's inauguration speech.
Lai used National Day speech to say Beijing has no right to represent Taiwan.
China launched Joint Sword 2024B exercises in response to Lai's National Day remarks.
Taiwan reported its largest Chinese naval deployment in nearly thirty years near the strait.
Taiwan said unannounced Chinese military activity wound down after roughly three days.
Pelosi landed in Taipei, becoming the highest-ranking US official to visit Taiwan in 25 years.
China fired ballistic missiles around Taiwan in unprecedented live-fire drills.
Beijing announced sanctions on Pelosi and suspended several climate and military talks with Washington.
China issued a white paper restating its goal of reunification with Taiwan.
Tsai met McCarthy at the Reagan Library in California, drawing Chinese condemnation.
China launched Joint Sword exercises simulating an encirclement of Taiwan.
Lai Ching-te transited the United States, prompting fresh Chinese military activity near Taiwan.
Lai Ching-te won Taiwan's presidential election in a three-way race.
The Pacific island nation Nauru ended diplomatic relations with Taiwan.
Taiwan's coast guard chase off Kinmen ended with two Chinese fishermen drowning.
The strongest earthquake in 25 years struck Taiwan, killing at least nine people.
Lai Ching-te took office as Taiwan's new president and called for cross-strait dialogue.
China encircled Taiwan with Joint Sword 2024A drills following Lai's inauguration speech.
Lai's National Day speech rejected Beijing's claim to represent the people of Taiwan.
China launched Joint Sword 2024B drills, again encircling Taiwan with naval and air forces.
Taiwan tracked roughly 90 Chinese naval ships in waters near the island.
China's apparent maritime show of force quieted without an official acknowledgement of drills.
